title: Palicourea bertieroides
intro: Palicourea bertieroides, also known as the "Cascalheira" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea bertieroides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the form of extracts and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Palicourea bertieroides belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Palicourea is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, as well as the Caribbean. Palicourea bertieroides is a species of shrub or small tree that is often found growing in moist, humid environments. The classification of Palicourea bertieroides places it within the Rubiaceae family, specifically within the subfamily Cinchonoideae. Further classification may involve the identification of its specific tribe, genus, and species within the Rubiaceae family.

side_effects: Palicourea bertieroides is a plant species used in traditional medicine, but its use may cause adverse effects. Palicourea bertieroides can lead to gastrointestinal problems, such as nausea and vomiting, due to its toxic compounds.
